ai_strategy_lead.name: UA AI Experience
ai_strategy_lead.title: Public AI strategy or initiative lead / hub identified from cited sources

ai_strategy.summary: University of Alabama has a public AI posture anchored in UA AI Experience. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: University of Alabama publishes AI policy or guidance with a permissive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

bodies.name: UA AI Experience
bodies.scope: Public AI strategy, guidance, teaching, research or technology coordination.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at University of Alabama is evidenced through UA AI Experience and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI policy and guidance
initiatives.description: University of Alabama publishes guidance or policy for responsible AI use, with attention to privacy, security, integrity, ethics, transparency and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete public signal of how the institution is translating AI into policy, teaching, research, operations, workforce preparation, or access.

investment.description: No public dollar amount disclosed for this initiative.

collaborations.description: No external collaboration identified in the cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets institutional guardrails for classroom, research, staff and administrative use.

initiatives.title: Alabama Center for the Advancement of Artificial Intelligence
initiatives.description: The ALA-AI Center unifies new and existing AI work across campus and is supported in part by a $2 million donation.
initiatives.significance: This closes a cost/funding gap with a disclosed AI-center gift and a named research structure.

investment.funding_source: Donation in honor of Dr. Marvin A. Griffin
investment.description: The new center is supported in part through a $2 million donation.

collaborations.description: No external collaboration identified in the cited source.

initiatives.title: HASTE AI assessment-redesign workshops
initiatives.description: UA Teaching Academy’s HASTE series trains educators to rebuild assignments and assessments for academic-integrity threats created by AI.
initiatives.significance: This adds direct assessment-redesign evidence to UA’s AI literacy posture.

investment.description: No public dollar amount disclosed for HASTE.

initiatives.policy_implications: Supports course and assessment redesign for AI-era academic integrity.

initiatives.title: High Performance Computing and Data Center for AI
initiatives.description: UA’s new $96 million HPC and Data Center is designed as a premier hub for AI, research computing, and industry collaboration, with leadership-class computing equipment planned.
initiatives.significance: This closes a compute and investment gap with a major disclosed infrastructure project.

investment.funding_source: NIST and Alabama Education Trust Fund support
investment.description: The facility is publicly described as a $96 million project and the construction approval article cites an estimated $25 million of computing power.

collaborations.description: Federal and state funding support the facility.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Responsible and inclusive AI access
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: The cited AI posture includes ethics, privacy, access, public-good, bias, workforce or broad literacy considerations.

regulatory_context.summary: No binding state higher-education AI policy was identified during this sweep; institutional guidance is the main policy evidence.

narrative_summary: University of Alabama has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is UA AI Experience, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.

gaps_acknowledged: Public sources did not identify a standalone chief AI officer role during this sweep.
Some AI initiatives did not disclose dollar amounts, user counts, or detailed implementation metrics.
Leadership names were not populated because the assigned task prioritized missing AI posture profiles and every non-obvious AI claim is URL-cited.
Worker 3 found major AI-center and HPC/data-center funding, but UA still does not publish enterprise AI platform seat counts or current GPU totals for UAHPC/CHPC.
UA AI Experience discloses broad enrollment plans for all students beginning Fall 2026, but no completion or outcome metrics are public yet.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
